## [00:00 - 03:02]

Let's talk about getting followers without writing tweets. And by tweets, I mean actual tweets that you post to your own timeline. So in other words, we could call this engagement. And there's a few things that you need to know that will truly help with your growth. So replies. And this is extremely important for accounts that are just starting out. Because think about it. If you're just writing tweets to your few 50 100 followers that are just old town friends, and they don't really they're not really following your new journey and things like that, you're not going to get any engagement, you're not going to get any retweets, it's going to be very hard to build an audience from your current connections alone. Okay. So what you need to do is find some big accounts in your niche. If you already follow one, just look at who they're following and go down the list, see who has a lot of followers, follow them. And then whenever they write a tweet, you want to comment under it, we'll go over some guidelines later. But for now, just keep in mind that you do not want to be a suck up, you don't want to just write, hey, like great tweet, awesome, great insights, stuff like that. You want to add your own thoughts to the tweet. What did you pull from the tweet? How did it help you? What can you add on to it? So write a tweet worthy reply, we'll learn how to tweet in a high engaging way later. But keep this in mind, you can write tweet worthy replies that expand upon the topic that they tweeted about, people will retweet you in the replies and you can retweet yourself so your followers can see your tweets. And now this one, I don't know if you should do this at a small account, I didn't do this as a small account, but trolling and having fun. I've realized lately that a lot of people can't tell when you're being sarcastic or just having fun on Twitter unless they're like friends with you unless you've connected with them in the DMs and they know that you're like a guy that likes to have fun. So if you're a big account and people know your intentions, then it's okay to troll and have fun and just like, like take a jab at your Twitter friends and stuff like that. So only troll and have fun if you know people will take it in the right way or else you're just setting yourself up for failure. And people won't understand you and they'll just think bad about you from the start. So just keep that in mind. And then when people comment on your own tweets, you want to respond to those followers in a timely manner to boost engagement on your post. This is more so for a higher follower account.
